preview_text:
The model favors Portugal at 35%, with Colombia trailing at 33% and a draw at 31.5%. This represents a tightly bunched outcome: all three results fall within a 4-percentage-point band, indicating substantial competitive balance. Portugal's marginal edge reflects only a modest model lean.

The narrow spread across win probabilities signals an evenly matched contest with genuine draw potential. A draw probability of 31.5% suggests roughly one in three matches with this profile ends level; neither team emerges as a clear control of play. Colombia and Portugal are separated by less than 2 percentage points, making the result genuinely uncertain on the model's reading.

The sportsbook consensus diverges noticeably from the model, rating Portugal's win probability at 44%—nearly 9 percentage points higher than the model's 35%. The market compresses Colombia's chances to 28% and the draw to 28.5%, suggesting sharper confidence in a Portugal victory. This gap does not indicate an error in either source; the dashboard makes no claim about which better captures true match likelihood.
